## Executive Summary
The HSCA witness interviews concerning Jack Ruby are mentioned in several documents, including a request for information on Ruby and a list of HSCA names that includes Ruby [3, 14]. However, the documents do not provide a comprehensive timeline of witness interviews specifically concerning Jack Ruby. The records do mention some interactions with Ruby, such as his interview with the FBI and his connections to other individuals [1, 9].

## Detailed Findings
### HSCA Requests and Names
The HSCA requested information on Jack Ruby, among other individuals, as part of their investigation [3]. The list of HSCA names includes Ruby, as well as other notable figures such as Lee Harvey Oswald, Marina Oswald, and Clay Shaw [6]. Another document mentions a search for records related to the JFK assassination, including files on Jack Ruby [7].

### Interactions with Ruby
Ruby was interviewed by the FBI, and he claimed to have no information concerning the assassination and did not know Lee Harvey Oswald or Jack Ruby [5]. Additionally, Ruby's background and associates are mentioned in a document that discusses his connections to organized crime [1].

### Witness Interviews and Testimony
While the documents do not provide a detailed timeline of witness interviews concerning Jack Ruby, they do mention some testimony and interviews related to the JFK assassination. For example, one document discusses the testimony of Captain Humes and his account of the autopsy [4]. Another document mentions the interview with Gary Edward Taylor, who discussed his employment and acquaintances [2].

### Gaps in the Record
The records do not directly describe a comprehensive timeline of HSCA witness interviews concerning Jack Ruby. However, they do mention some interactions with Ruby and his connections to other individuals, as well as some testimony and interviews related to the JFK assassination [1, 3, 9].
